blockingIssues.description: pyproject.toml does not satisfy the feature's explicit `pybaseball>=2.3` dependency requirement; it installs pybaseball from GitHub HEAD, and the current environment reports version 2.2.7.
fetch_mlb_odds() silently drops events when it cannot resolve a pre-seeded `games` row, so a fresh database can return no odds snapshots for real API data.
Odds snapshot inserts and monthly usage updates are committed only after the full event loop, so one later event failure can roll back earlier successful writes and undercount quota usage.

fetch_fielding_stats() persists only qualified fielders because it calls Fangraphs fielding_stats() without overriding the default qualification filter, rather than storing the raw defensive dataset required by the feature.
fetch_catcher_framing() persists only qualified catchers because pybaseball's default framing qualification threshold is left in place, so raw framing data is incomplete.
fetch_confirmed_lineups(date) ignores the requested date when merging projected scraping sources, so non-today requests can attach the wrong projected lineups and starter changes.
The advertised RotoBaller fallback is implemented with a brittle regex-based HTML parser and lacks direct success-path test coverage, so the required fallback contract is not reliably met.
Weather cache freshness uses the forecast slot timestamp as `fetched_at` instead of the actual API retrieval time, allowing stale cached weather to survive beyond the intended 6-hour TTL.

appliedUpdates.description: Added Windows-safe install/test/typecheck commands plus explicit Ruff lint commands so future workers have manifest-backed validation options in PowerShell.
Updated `.factory/library/environment.md` with the Odds API events/per-event-odds flow and quota-header accounting details used by the current odds client.
Updated `.factory/library/environment.md` with the current pybaseball version mismatch note and a PowerShell-specific `py_compile` caveat.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Replace the PowerShell-fragile `python -m py_compile src/**/*.py` guidance with Windows-aware validation commands or an explicit pointer to `.factory/services.yaml` / `.factory/library/environment.md` for platform-safe equivalents.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features t1-project-scaffolding, t3-sqlite-schema, t4-pydantic-models, t6-pybaseball-ingestion, t7-mlb-lineup-client, and t8-weather-client all recorded justified deviations because the current Windows environment could not reliably use the documented glob-based validation/init flow.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit exception path for first-pass scaffolding/bootstrap features so workers can document why strict red-phase TDD and baseline validator requirements are not always possible before the repo exists.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: t1-project-scaffolding had to deviate from the default tests-first/bootstrap procedure because the repository, test harness, and packaging files were being created for the first time.

rejectedObservations.observation: Repeated notes that `.factory/services.yaml` and `.factory/init.sh` use Windows-fragile commands.
rejectedObservations.reason: deduplicated and partially addressed: services.yaml received additive Windows-safe commands, while the remaining init.sh mismatch was left for orchestrator follow-up rather than patched here.

rejectedObservations.observation: The lineup-client review's note that public projected lineup pages are daily/current-day sources rather than date-parameterized APIs.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected for shared-state application because it is currently an implementation-specific failure mode rather than broad guidance that is safe to codify before the feature is fixed.
